objective: Localize the single ~18.72 GiB non-Thompson allocation that binds the v0.15 1km grids (167,904 / 204,800 cols) above the ~125,928-col ceiling, then fix-or-scope.
headline: The binding allocation is NOT a single un-tiled physics/dycore array. It is XLA's coalesced `preallocated-temp` scratch arena for the fused `_run_forecast_operational_jit` program. Buffer-assignment, XLA rematerialization, and the live bfc OOM all agree. There is no un-tiled g-point / dycore transient to column-tile away; the existing per-column scan-tile pattern cannot reduce a fused-program live-set.
method: PHASE A (authoritative): compiled the FULL operational pipeline (real Canairy d02 case: mp=8 Thompson, pbl=5 MYNN, sfclay=5, cu=0, ra_sw/lw=4 RRTMG, diff_opt=1 km_opt=4 2D-Smagorinsky) at the base grid (66x159=10,494 cols) with XLA HLO + buffer-assignment dumping. Parsed module_0951.jit__run_forecast_operational_jit buffer-assignment for the largest allocations; scaled linearly to 167,904 cols (linear in ncol, R2 0.997 per grid_scaling.json).
PHASE B (corroboration): attempted the 167,904-col (132x1272) forecast under the platform/bfc allocator to capture XLA's own memory diagnostics.

buffer_assignment_top_allocations_base_g.kind: preallocated-temp (XLA coalesced scratch arena for the whole fused module)
parameter 47 f64[25,4,5,45,160] (static radiation/boundary table, NOT ncol-scaling)

evidence.gap_note: The #1 allocation (1.356 GiB) is ~50x larger than #2 (0.027 GiB). The arena is the ONLY large allocation; everything else is tiny static tables or single 2D/3D fields. There is no second giant.

arena_composition.value_shapes: full-grid fp64 fields: f64[44,66,159] (961 occ), f64[45,66,159] (313), f64[44,66,160] (93) and their RK/acoustic carry tuples
arena_composition.interpretation: arena = ~394 coexisting full-grid (nz,ny,nx) fp64 temporaries at peak. The ship-gate signature 'nz x 340 = 14,960 elems/col' IS this live-field count (cuda_async measured ~340; bfc buffer-assignment shows ~394 -- same phenomenon, allocator-dependent rounding). It is NOT one array with a 340-wide dimension.

xla_rematerialization_167904col.message: Can't reduce memory use below 21.60GiB (23191710308 bytes) by rematerialization; only reduced to 21.74GiB (23339968441 bytes), down from 23.36GiB (25080307317 bytes) originally
xla_rematerialization_167904col.meaning: XLA's OWN compute-for-memory pass already ran and proved the fused dycore live-set is irreducible by recomputation below ~21.6 GiB. This is an independent lower bound on the working-set floor at fp64.

live_bfc_oom_167904col.message: Allocator (GPU_0_bfc) ran out of memory trying to allocate 18.51GiB (rounded to 19874694656) requested by op
live_bfc_oom_167904col.meaning: The 18.51 GiB single allocation request = the preallocated-temp arena. Matches the ship-gate's '18.72 GiB single non-Thompson allocation' (cuda_async). With ~6-26 GiB already resident (operational state + tables), this exceeds the 32 GiB RTX 5090 -> OOM.

excluded_hypotheses.rrtmg_gpoint_transient: REFUTED. RRTMG SW (_shortwave_column_tiled_impl, rrtmg_sw.py:2273) AND LW (_longwave_column_tiled_impl, rrtmg_lw.py:2627) are BOTH column-tiled (default ON, 16384-col tiles). Outputs are (ncol,nz)-class flux/heating arrays (~9 fields). The big pre-fusion g-point shapes (f64[10494,44,48], f64[10494,44,3,16]) appear only INSIDE the scan body as per-tile work and are NOT live top-level buffers (0 occurrences as allocations in the final buffer-assignment).
excluded_hypotheses.mynn_boulac_dense_matrix: REFUTED. step_mynn_pbl_column -> _tiled_mynn_step (mynn_pbl.py:1574) runs the (B,nz,nz) BouLac matrices inside a 16384-col scan-tile. f64[10494,44,44] appears only as a transient packed/reused inside the arena (3 value-refs), not a standalone 9.9 GiB buffer.
excluded_hypotheses.thompson_mp_arena: REFUTED (and out of scope/sibling-owned). Thompson is column-tiled (thompson_column.py:1862). MP adapters produce (ncol,nz) fields only.
excluded_hypotheses.cumulus_gf_tiedtke_vmap: REFUTED for this case. The real Canairy d02 namelist has cu_physics=0 (only d01=1); no cumulus scheme runs on the probed domain.
excluded_hypotheses.acoustic_scan_trajectory_materialization: REFUTED. The acoustic lax.scan (operational_mode.py:2029) returns None as the stacked output (xs=None, second carry element None) and _acoustic_unroll() defaults to 1 -- only the final carry is kept, no (K,nz,ny,nx) trajectory is materialized.
excluded_hypotheses.single_giant_dycore_array: REFUTED. No single allocation exceeds 1.356 GiB at base. The 'giant' is the COALESCED arena of ~394 ordinary full-grid fields, not one array.

tileability_classification.verdict: NOT tileable with the existing per-column scan-tile pattern (column_tiling.tiled_column_apply / RRTMG / MYNN).
why: The binding allocation is not a single op -- it is the union of ~394 distinct full-grid fp64 temporaries that XLA holds live simultaneously across the fused RK3 stages + acoustic substeps + 2D-Smagorinsky horizontal diffusion (km_opt=4) + advection + lateral-boundary + halo + physics-merge (which also holds the full `physical_origin` State snapshot live for the whole step).

The dominant ops are HORIZONTALLY COUPLED (advection/diffusion stencils, halo exchange, lateral boundaries). tiled_column_apply assumes per-column independence; applying it to these would be NON-value-identical (it would drop the horizontal-neighbour contributions at tile edges). That violates the V0150-TIERED-IDENTITY contract.

XLA rematerialization (the compiler's built-in memory-vs-compute optimizer) already ran and could not push the live-set below 21.6 GiB -- the working-set floor of the fp64 dycore program is intrinsic, not a missing-tile bug.

faithful_fix_options_for_manager.saving: halves the arena: ~21.7 -> ~10.8 GiB @167,904, which would fit all named 1km targets
faithful_fix_options_for_manager.effort: LARGE -- this is the separate fp64-operational ADR lane (V0150 top lever per memory), a pervasive precision change requiring a tiered-identity re-gate across all schemes. NOT a column-tile.
faithful_fix_options_for_manager.owner: fp64-operational-state ADR lane, not this VRAM-tile task

faithful_fix_options_for_manager.option: spatial (domain) decomposition of the whole dycore with halos
faithful_fix_options_for_manager.saving: caps the per-shard live-set to a sub-domain
faithful_fix_options_for_manager.effort: VERY LARGE -- a fundamentally different decomposition than column-tiling: requires halo-exchange-correct sharded advection/diffusion/acoustic; this is the multi-GPU / sharded-horizontal lane (sharded_horizontal.py exists but is not the single-GPU operational path).

faithful_fix_options_for_manager.option: shrink the live-set op-by-op (e.g. avoid materializing the full physical_origin snapshot; fuse/donate more aggressively)
faithful_fix_options_for_manager.saving: each removed full-grid field ~0.077 GiB @167,904; would need ~140 fields removed to reach the target -- not feasible from micro-opts since most fields are genuinely needed across the step
faithful_fix_options_for_manager.effort: MEDIUM-LARGE, LOW yield, HIGH regression risk on a bit-identity-gated dycore

recommendation: v0.15 cannot ship a default-capability 1km/large-grid headline on a single 32 GiB RTX 5090: the binding constraint is the intrinsic fp64 working-set of the fused dycore (~21.7 GiB irreducible @167,904), not a missing column-tile. The largest stable single-GPU grid remains ~125,928 cols (per the ship-gate). The real unlock is the fp64->fp32 operational-state ADR (halves the working set) and/or sharded-horizontal multi-GPU -- both are their own milestones, out of scope for the v0.15 kernel-final close. Document large-grid 1km as a next-version lane.
